content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_constraint; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` run logs must not disappear silently.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory data model layered architecture; The `v3` memory data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by separating memory into layers: `Source` as evidence, `Entity`/`Claim` as graph-shaped semantics, `Note` as rich intermediate context, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode` as operational projections, and `MemoryRun` as audit.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing the overall v3 memory data model architecture.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, Profile is not the source of truth.; scope=Applies to Profile in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, controversial facts should be left in Claim or Note rather than pulled into Profile.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, if meaning is still too soft, Note should be preferred over a bad Claim.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> needed_for; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see which model was used in a memory pipeline run.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> needed_for; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see exactly what was retrieved in a memory pipeline run.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> needed_for; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see what budget was allocated in a memory pipeline run.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> needed_for; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see where the memory pipeline broke.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> needed_for; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see which operations were actually applied in a memory pipeline run.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> needed_for; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see which operations were proposed in a memory pipeline run.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> needed_for; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `MemoryRun` is needed to see which prompt ran in a memory pipeline run.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Note as intermediate semantic memory fragment; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment for important, retrieval-useful context that should not yet be cemented as a hard fact; typical uses include design direction, decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining Note purpose and use cases.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Entity as canonical memory node with aliases and reversible merges; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Entity` is the canonical memory node used to unify different mentions of the same object and anchor claims, tasks, notes, and episodes. It supports aliases and merge while preserving history: aliases must point to the current canonical entity, and mistaken merges must be reversible through an audit trail.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the Entity component role, aliases, merge behavior, and invariants.
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, a Claim must make sense without the original chat.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, explicit facts should be preferred over inference in Profile.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, explicit statements should be preferred over inference for Claims.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<14> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, one Claim must equal one predicate application.; scope=Applies to Claim invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<15> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, Profile must be rebuildable.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<16> has_constraint; In the v3 memory data model, Profile must be short.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`.

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Source; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=Source; reason=Mention refers to the existing Source concept in the memory data model.
- mention=Entity; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<1>; type=existing; name=Entity; reason=Mention refers to the existing Entity concept in the memory data model.
- mention=Note; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<2>; type=existing; name=Note; reason=Mention refers to the existing Note concept in the memory data model.
- mention=Claim; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<3>; type=existing; name=Claim; reason=Mention refers to the existing Claim concept in the memory data model.
- mention=Profile; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<4>; type=existing; name=Profile; reason=Mention refers to the existing Profile concept in the memory data model.
- mention=Task; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Task (memory data model); reason=Task is listed as a memory data model concept and no existing Task candidate is available.
- mention=Episode; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<6>; type=existing; name=Episode; reason=Mention refers to the existing Episode concept in the memory data model.
- mention=MemoryRun; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<7>; type=existing; name=MemoryRun; reason=Mention refers to the existing MemoryRun concept in the memory data model.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<8>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Модель данных в одной строке` defines the compact roles of the main memory entities: `Source` records what was said or observed, `Entity` identifies who or what something is, `Note` stores important useful context not yet cemented as fact, `Claim` stores normalized facts, `Profile` is a compact projection of stable context, `Task` records what must be done, `Episode` records what the system learned, and `MemoryRun` records exactly how memory was updated.","tags":["doc_digest","data_model","memory_entities"],"title":"One-line roles of v3 memory model entities","valid_from":null,"valid_to":null}]}
